purpose: Phase 1 smoke set for baseline metrics. Not the golden set (which lands after Phase 1 close per tests/golden/CURATION.md). 15 queries against 8 seed docs, each query maps to exactly one expected doc.

docs.title: BGE-M3 multilingual embedding model reference
docs.content: BGE-M3 is a multilingual embedding model produced by BAAI. It outputs 1024-dimensional dense vectors and supports an 8192-token context window. Unlike most embedders it can produce dense, sparse, and ColBERT-style multi-vector outputs from one model. License is MIT.

docs.title: Snowflake arctic-embed-l v2.0 reference
docs.content: Snowflake arctic-embed-l v2.0 is an English-leaning embedding model with 568 million parameters and 1024-dimensional output. It is the rag-base default but is being replaced by BGE-M3 for multilingual coverage.

docs.title: BGE-reranker-v2-m3 cross-encoder reranker
docs.content: BGE-reranker-v2-m3 is a multilingual cross-encoder reranker from BAAI built on a MiniLM-class backbone with about 568 million parameters. BEIR NDCG@10 around 51.8 on the standard split. The default rag-base reranker before the Phase 4 swap.

docs.title: Mixedbread mxbai-rerank-large-v2 cross-encoder
docs.content: mxbai-rerank-large-v2 is the Apache 2.0 reranker from Mixedbread released March 2025. 1.5 billion parameters, BEIR average NDCG@10 of 57.49. The Phase 4 SOTA pick for self-hosted reranking in rag-base.

docs.title: LightRAG dual-level retrieval architecture
docs.content: LightRAG generates two query keys per question: a low-level entity-oriented key and a high-level theme-oriented key. Both are matched against entity and relation vector stores; one-hop neighbors in the graph are fetched and assembled into LLM context. Modes are naive, local, global, hybrid, and mix.

docs.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion with k equals 60
docs.content: Reciprocal Rank Fusion combines multiple ranked lists using score equals sum of one over k plus rank. The constant k equals 60 traces to Cormack 2009 and is the default in Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Weaviate, MariaDB, and Vertex AI hybrid search.

docs.title: ParadeDB pg_search BM25 extension for Postgres
docs.content: ParadeDB pg_search embeds Tantivy inside Postgres to provide real BM25 scoring as an alternative to tsvector ts_rank. Tiger Data pg_textsearch reports 2.4 to 6.5 times faster than ParadeDB at 138 million document scale on 2 to 4 term queries.

docs.title: Anthropic Contextual Retrieval per-chunk LLM blurb
docs.content: Anthropic Contextual Retrieval prepends a 50 to 100 token LLM-generated blurb to each chunk before embedding and BM25 indexing. Originally reduced top-20 retrieval failures by 35 percent with embeddings alone, 49 percent with BM25, 67 percent with reranking. Cost is roughly 1 dollar per million document tokens with prompt caching.
